name: grep
description: Search file contents by regular expression across the workspace. Returns matching lines with path and 1-based line number, best for finding where a symbol/string is used. Respects credential and vendored-tree exclusions. Prefer this over `bash grep` — it stays inside the workspace boundary, redacts credentials, and works even when network egress is contained.

pattern.description: Go (RE2) regular expression to match against each line.

path.description: Optional file or subdirectory (workspace-relative) to limit the search. Defaults to the whole workspace.

glob.description: Optional filename glob to restrict which files are searched by basename, e.g. "*.go" or "*.{ts,tsx}".

output_mode.description: "content" (default) returns matching lines; "files_with_matches" returns only the paths that matched (cheaper); "count" returns per-file match counts.

max_results.description: Maximum matching lines to return in content mode (default 100, cap 200).
